concerns.text: Task T027 implements 'SC-006: Theoretical Efficiency Validation' (comparing FLOPs/Rank against baselines). However, SC-006 is defined in plan.md but has NO corresponding Functional Requirement (FR) in spec.md. Implementing a task for a requirement that does not exist in the spec violates the constraint that tasks must not introduce scope beyond the spec. The spec must be updated to include FR-006 or the task must be removed.

Task T007 ('simulate_step.py') generates 'artifacts/delta_w.pt' to satisfy FR-001/FR-002. However, FR-001 and FR-002 in spec.md mandate executing 'svd.py' and 'upd_rank.py' on the 'gsm8k' dataset, not on a simulated delta. The spec does not explicitly authorize a 'simulated training step' as a valid input for these scripts; it implies running the scripts on actual data or a pre-existing delta. This task silently relaxes the requirement to 'run on real data' to 'run on simulated data', violating the 'Real-Call Testing' principle (Constitution Principle V) and the specific FR text.

Task T013 attempts to fetch the EffOPD submodule from 'projects/PROJ-597-https-arxiv-org-abs-2605-11739/external/EffOPD/'. This path is a mangled URL string, not a valid git repository path. This task cannot execute as written, meaning the deliverable (submodule presence) will fail, violating the acceptance criteria in US-1 which requires the submodule to exist. The task description must be corrected to a valid git URL or relative path.

Task T005 implements 'memory_guard.py' to enforce '≤7 GB RAM usage'. FR-005 requires the system to 'sample the dataset to ≤7 GB total memory usage'. The task description implies a hard runtime guard (killing the process if >7GB), whereas the spec requires a proactive sampling strategy. If the task implements a runtime kill-switch instead of a pre-loading sampler, it fails to satisfy the 'sampling' requirement of FR-005, constituting a semantic weakening of the constraint.
